title: Translation unit and main deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Translation unit and main: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? C programs may use void main because ignoring a result is equivalent to returning a valid termination status.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.
C programs may use void main because ignoring a result is equivalent to returning a valid termination status.

answer.explanation: For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.

items.title: Translation unit and main: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing C checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: C programs may use void main because ignoring a result is equivalent to returning a valid termination status.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'int main(void)'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact C token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.template: For a portable hosted program, define {{key-rule}} or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.

answer.explanation: For a portable hosted program, define int main(void) or a supported argument form and return an integer status; void main is not a portable hosted definition.

items.title: Translation unit and main: executable application
items.prompt: Read one integer in a conforming hosted C program, print the integer plus one followed by a newline, and return a successful integer status.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
